item.title: The Hardest Thing
item.description:
The hardest thing I have ever had to do in my life was saying goodbye to my 7 year old Sheltie, Nihla. Much too late we found out that she was in the advanced stages of chronic kidney failure. She was too young to suffer this fate, and certainly to young to be taken away from us. I was, and remain, unprepared to deal with this.

It seems as though one moment she was fine, her normal self, and the next moment we were having to make an end-of-life decision. Too soon, and far too sudden. I continue to be shocked by her absence. Every day I imagine that I just need to open the front door and there you will be, waiting for me to let you in the house.

We all miss you Nihla. I can feel your fuzzy noggin under my hand.
item.dc:creator: Mark E. Drummond

item.title: New Squat PR
item.description:
We just completed a cycle of volume work at the gym. This week we are chasing new PRs. I haven’t attempted a new squat PR since March when I managed 290lbs. Even then, 290lbs is only a recent PR, not my all-time best.

On Monday I hit 315lbs which is both a recent and all time PR, so I am pretty happy about that. The lift felt good and I’m sure I could manage 325lbs or more. There is a faint glimmer at the end of a long tunnel that looks like a 405lb squat.

Looking forward to a new Clean PR. Like my squat, it has been a while, but I have a shot at finally making 100kg. As long as I have the, and please excuse the vernacular, testicular fortitude to get under the bar.
item.dc:creator: Mark E. Drummond

item.title: The First (real) Post
item.description:
Welcome!

My name is Mark. I’m a career IT systems analyst edging my way toward a second career in data analysis. In recent years I’ve been looking for a new challenge on my career path. I’ve also been working on an undergraduate degree in Economics, gearing my courses towards econometrics, computing and mathematics.

Combining my IT systems background, my academic studies, some excellent data related MOOCs and an opportunity to work with the nascent data team where I work, I have an exciting opportunity to turn this into something real.

I hope to use this site to record much of my journey from IT Systems Analyst to Data Analyst, highlighting some of my work in an online portfolio. If you are interested in some background information, please visit The About Page.
item.dc:creator: Mark E. Drummond
